core_mechanism: Bayesian spike-and-slab sparse selection over a large dictionary of candidate components: each candidate is assigned a two-component mixture prior (a point-mass 'spike' at zero versus a diffuse 'slab'), and a stochastic search through model space yields per-candidate posterior inclusion probabilities, enforcing sparsity while quantifying each component's relevance.
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on a 'structured spike-and-slab prior' over a refined grid of candidate frequencies explored by a 'stochastic search algorithm' that outputs 'posterior inclusion probabilities' to promote sparsity, which is exactly a mixture-prior sparse-selection mechanism independent of the frequencies being the objects selected.
